slides.content:
вҖў Pattern Representation Learning

вҖ“ Support Vector Machines (week 11)
вҖў Deep Learning (week 12)
вҖў Revision and Q&A (week 13)

slides.title: Pattern Representation Learning
slides.content:
вһў We will discuss:
вһў What is pattern representation learning
вһў Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
вһў Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF)
вһў At the end of this lecture, you should be able to:
вһў Know what pattern representation learning is
вһў Understand rationales behind PCA and NMF
вһў Perform PCA and NMF
5

slides.title: What are we doing with Pattern Recognition?
slides.content:
Raw Data
Data Preprocessing
Grayscale Normalization Augmentation
Extract Informative Face Neck Legs
Pattern Representation Horse Long Long Long
Cat Round Short Short
Unsupervised Supervised
K-means
PR Model (after training) Boosting
Hierarchical clustering
Support Vector Machine
Gaussian Mixture Model
Deep Neural Network
Recognition Results
Cluster assignment Classification / Regression
6

slides.title: Representation Learning / Extraction
slides.content:
вҖў Representation learning refers to transforming the
raw/preprocessed data into another (possibly lower-dimensional)
space.
вҖў Such that, in the new space, one can perform pattern recognition
more easily.
вҖў Criterion for good representation in different problem settings:
вҖ“ Unsupervised: minimize information loss (no class information)
вҖ“ Supervised: maximize discrimination (with class information)
Face Neck Legs
Horse Long Long Long
Cat Round Short Short
7

slides.title: Why Feature Extraction?
slides.content:
вҖў Many traditional pattern recognition techniques may
not be effective for high-dimensional data
вҖ“ Curse of dimensionality
8

вҖў Patterns may have small intrinsic dimension
Intrinsic
dimensions
(50-100
dimensions)
400x400x3=480000
dimensions
9

вҖў Visualization: projecting high-dimensional data onto
2D or 3D planes
вҖў Data compression: efficient storage and retrieval
вҖў Noise removal: positive effect on testing accuracy
10

slides.title: Applications of Feature Extraction
slides.content:
вҖў Face recognition
вҖў Handwritten digit recognition
вҖў Text mining
вҖў Image retrieval
Text
вҖў Protein classification
Handwritten digit
Image database
Proteins
11

slides.title: Unsupervised Feature Learning
slides.content: Principal Component Analysis

slides.title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
slides.content:
вҖў Probably the most widely-used and
well-known multivariate analysis
method.
вҖў Introduced by Pearson (1901)
вҖў First applied in ecology by Goodall
(1954) under the name вҖңfactor analysisвҖқ.
Karl Pearson
Pearson, K. (1901). "On Lines and Planesof Closest Fit to Systems of Points in Space"
PhilosophicalMagazine 2 (11): 559вҖ“572.
13

slides.title: What is Principal Component Analysis?
slides.content:
вҖў Principal component analysis (PCA)
вҖ“ Reduce the dimensionality of a collection of observations by
projecting onto a new smaller set of variables, called principal
components (PCs), while preserving as much information as
possible.
y
вҖў What is PC?
вҖ“ Capture the big (principal) variability in the data
вҖ“ Ordered by captured variations from largest to smallest (e.g., 1st PC
captures the largest variability)
вҖ“ PCs are uncorrelated (orthogonal to each other)
14

slides.title: PCA and Classification
slides.content:
вҖў Classification with PCA
вҖ“ Compute PCs using training dataset
вҖ“ Project both training and testing data into the obtained PCs space
вҖ“ For each testing data point, use K-Nearest-Neighbor (KNN) for classification
вҖ“ Issue: accuracy is sensitive to the number of PCs
Testing data point
How to determine
the number of PCs?
To choose p based on percentage of variation to
retain, we can use the following criterion (smallest p):
рқ‘қ
Пғ рқңҶ
1 рқ‘–
вүҘ рқ‘Үв„Һрқ‘ҹрқ‘’рқ‘ в„Һрқ‘ңрқ‘ҷрқ‘‘ (рқ‘’. рқ‘”. , 0.95)
рқ‘‘
Пғ рқңҶ
1 рқ‘–
32

slides.title: PCA Remarks
slides.content:
вҖў PCA
вҖ“ finds orthonormal basis for data
вҖ“ Sorts dimensions in order of вҖңimportanceвҖқ
вҖ“ Discard low significance dimensions
вҖў Uses:
вҖ“ Get compact description
вҖ“ Ignore noise
вҖ“ Improve classification (hopefully)
33

slides.title: PCA Limitations
slides.content:
вҖў PCA is not always an optimal feature extraction for classification
вҖ“ PCA is based on the sample covariance, irrespective of class-membership
вҖ“ The projection axes chosen by PCA might not provide good discrimination power
34

вҖў PCA cannot capture NON-LINEAR structure
Note: Curvilinear Component Analysis can solve this case. Study this work
if you are interested.
35

вҖў Lack of interpretability
- Each PC projection is a linear combination of all input features
рқ‘Ү
рқ‘Ұ = рқҗі рқҗұ
рқ‘ҳ рқ‘ҳ
What does each PC actually represent?
e.g., Face Recognition
Not localized to a single feature
such as вҖңmouthвҖқ, вҖңeyebrowsвҖқ
e.g., Topic Modelling from Text
A PC projection is a linear combination of occurrence of many words

slides.title: Unsupervised Feature Learning
slides.content:
Non-negative Matrix Factorization
(non-negative coefficients and basis vectors)
38

slides.title: NMF: Motivation
slides.content:
вҖў Non-negative coefficients and basis vectors
-More intuitive for non-negative datasets (e.g., images, word counts in
documents, etc.)
-Leads to basis vectors that represent parts
Basis images Reconstructed
39
